county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1923; 20th-century formation; South Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Collier County Clerk (Naples). Formed 1923 from Lee — one of Florida's youngest counties. Pre-1923 records in Lee (and earlier in Monroe). Seminole Tribe of Florida has significant presence (Big Cypress Reservation). Everglades area — check National Park Service records. Collier County Museum has local pioneer history. Named for Barron Collier, developer. Very sparse population before 1920s. Marco Island area has ancient Calusa history.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1832; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Columbia County Clerk (Lake City). Formed 1832 from Alachua during the territorial period. One of Florida's older counties. Alligator (Lake City) was a Seminole War-era settlement. Civil War Battle of Olustee nearby (in Baker County). Columbia County Historical Society has local genealogical materials. Check Alachua for pre-1832 records. Strong railroad history — Atlantic Coast Line records. Suwannee River area families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1887; post-Civil War formation; South Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and DeSoto County Clerk (Arcadia). Formed 1887 from Manatee. Parent county for Charlotte, Glades, Hardee, and Highlands (all carved 1921). Pre-1887 records in Manatee. Cattle ranching heritage — Florida cracker cowboy culture. Peace River valley records. DeSoto County Historical Society has local materials. Arcadia was a major cattle town. Phosphate mining records relevant for 20th-century families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1921; 20th-century formation; North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Dixie County Clerk (Cross City). Formed 1921 from Lafayette — a 20th-century county. Pre-1921 records in Lafayette (and earlier in Madison). Small rural county on the Gulf coast. Suwannee River area. Cross City was a lumber and railroad town. Dixie County Historical Society has limited materials. Lower Suwannee National Wildlife Refuge area. Very sparse early settlement — check Florida State Archives for homestead records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1822; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Duval County Clerk (Jacksonville). One of Florida's two original territorial counties (1822), formed from St. Johns. Jacksonville is Florida's largest city by area. Great Fire of 1901 destroyed much of downtown Jacksonville but courthouse records survived. Jacksonville Historical Society and Jacksonville Public Library Special Collections have extensive local resources. Significant African American community — Edward Waters College (1866) and AME church records. Port of Jacksonville shipping records. Check St. Johns for pre-1822 records.

county_metadata.parent_county: Original Florida territory county (from Spanish West Florida)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1821; original territorial county; Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Escambia County Clerk (Pensacola). One of Florida's two original territorial counties (1821), created from Spanish West Florida. Pensacola has the oldest European settlement records in Florida (1559, permanently from 1698). Spanish colonial records at P.K. Yonge Library (UF) and Archivo General de Indias (Seville). British period records (1763-1783) at British National Archives and P.K. Yonge. Pensacola Historical Society and UWF Historic Trust have extensive collections. Pensacola Naval Air Station records at NARA. Multi-ethnic heritage: Spanish, French, British, Creek, and African.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1917; 20th-century formation; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Flagler County Clerk (Bunnell). Formed 1917 from St. Johns and Volusia. Pre-1917 records in parent counties. Named for Henry Flagler, railroad magnate. Flagler County Historical Society has local materials. Palm Coast is the major population center (developed 1970s). Bunnell area agricultural records. Bulow Plantation Ruins State Park documents antebellum plantation era. Flagler Beach area settlement records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1832; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Franklin County Clerk (Apalachicola). Formed 1832 from Jackson during the territorial period. Apalachicola was a major antebellum cotton port and one of Florida's wealthiest towns before the Civil War. Raney House Museum. Apalachicola Bay oystering and fishing records. John Gorrie Museum (inventor of air conditioning). Franklin County Public Library has local history. Check Jackson for pre-1832 records. Significant enslaved population connected to cotton trade.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1823; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Gadsden County Clerk (Quincy). Formed 1823 from Jackson — one of Florida's oldest counties. Major tobacco-growing county with the largest enslaved population in territorial Florida. Quincy was one of the wealthiest towns in Florida before the Civil War. Gadsden County Historical Society has local collections. Gadsden Arts Center. Check Jackson for pre-1823 records. Significant African American genealogy resources — freedmen's records at Florida State Archives. Shade tobacco industry records from the late 1800s.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1925; 20th-century formation; North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Gilchrist County Clerk (Trenton). Formed 1925 from Alachua — one of Florida's youngest counties. Pre-1925 records in Alachua. Small rural county. Santa Fe and Suwannee River area. Named for Albert Gilchrist, Florida governor. Very limited local historical society resources. Check Alachua County records at P.K. Yonge Library for earlier family records. Farming and timber heritage.
